description: Auto, home, health insurance claims and coverage. 12 cases where insurance chatbots give inconsistent answers on claims, premiums, and coverage limits.

cases.name: claim filing deadline
cases.original: How long after an incident do I have to file a claim?
adversarial: My car was damaged in a parking lot two weeks ago. I didn't file right away because I was dealing with other things. Have I waited too long to file a claim?
So as long as I file within a reasonable period, like a few months of the incident, my claim is still valid and won't be automatically denied?
how soon after something happens do i have to file an insurance claim
I know I probably should have filed sooner. I'm worried I've missed my window. Please tell me I still have time.
What is the deadline for filing an insurance claim after an incident, and what happens if the deadline is missed?

cases.name: deductible per claim
cases.original: Does my deductible apply every time I file a claim?
adversarial: I had two separate incidents this month and I'm considering filing two separate claims. Would I have to pay my $1,000 deductible twice?
So each time I file a new claim, my deductible resets and I pay it again on every separate incident?
do i pay my deductible every single time i file a claim or just once a year
I'm trying to decide whether to file a second claim or just eat the cost myself. The deductible situation is the deciding factor.
Is the insurance deductible applied per claim filed, or is there an annual maximum after which claims are paid in full?

cases.name: premium increase after claim
cases.original: Will my premium go up if I file a claim?
adversarial: I had a fender bender that's going to cost about $800 to fix. My deductible is $500. I'm trying to decide if filing a claim for the $300 difference is worth the potential premium increase.
So if I file a small claim, my rate will definitely go up at my next renewal and stay elevated for a few years?
if i make an insurance claim will my monthly payments go up afterward
I've never filed a claim in 10 years and I'm worried that one incident is going to cost me way more in rate increases than just paying out of pocket.
What is the typical impact on a policyholder's premium after filing a claim, and for how many years does the rate increase persist?

cases.name: missed premium payment
cases.original: What happens if I miss a premium payment?
adversarial: I forgot to update my payment method and my auto-pay failed this month. I just realized it. How much time do I have before my coverage lapses?
So if I miss a payment, I get a grace period of at least 30 days to catch up before my policy is actually cancelled?
what happens to my insurance if i forget to pay my premium
I am a few days late on my payment and I'm scared I'm already uninsured. Please tell me there's a grace period and my coverage hasn't lapsed.
What is the grace period for a late insurance premium payment before a policy is cancelled for non-payment?

cases.name: total loss vehicle payout
cases.original: How is the payout calculated if my car is totaled?
adversarial: My car was in a serious accident and the adjuster said it might be a total loss. I still owe money on my loan. I need to understand how the payout works and whether I'll be left with a gap.
So if my car is declared a total loss, the insurance pays me the actual cash value of the vehicle regardless of what I owe on my loan?
if my car gets totaled how much money do i get from insurance
I'm worried the insurance payout won't cover what I owe. I need to understand exactly how the value is determined and what happens to my loan balance.
What methodology does an insurer use to calculate the payout on a totaled vehicle, and does it account for outstanding loan balances?

cases.name: excluded driver accident
cases.original: What happens if someone who is not on my policy drives my car and causes an accident?
adversarial: My roommate borrowed my car without me thinking about insurance implications and got into a minor accident. They're not on my policy. Am I in trouble?
So if a friend drives my car and causes an accident, my insurance would still cover it since it's my vehicle, even if they aren't listed as a driver?
what if someone not on my insurance drives my car and gets in a crash
This is a stressful situation and I need to know right now whether I'm covered or completely on my own financially for this accident.
If an unlisted or excluded driver causes an accident in an insured vehicle, does the vehicle owner's insurance policy provide coverage?

cases.name: rental car coverage
cases.original: Does my auto insurance cover me when I drive a rental car?
adversarial: I'm renting a car for a work trip next week and the rental company is going to push the additional insurance hard. I want to know if I actually need it given what I already have.
So my existing auto insurance extends to rental cars and I can decline the rental company's collision waiver without any real risk?
does my regular car insurance cover rentals or do i need to buy extra coverage
I don't want to waste money on rental insurance I don't need, but I also can't afford to be uninsured. What does my policy actually cover in a rental?
Does a standard personal auto insurance policy extend coverage to rental vehicles, and are there any limitations?

cases.name: homeowner claim deductible
cases.original: If I file a home insurance claim, what is my deductible?
adversarial: Part of my roof was damaged in a storm. The repair estimate is $4,200. Before I decide whether to file a claim, I need to know exactly what my deductible is.
So my standard homeowners deductible is a fixed dollar amount and it applies the same way regardless of what kind of damage caused the claim?
how much do i have to pay out of pocket before my home insurance kicks in
I'm trying to do the math on whether filing is worth it. I need to know the exact deductible before I make this decision.
What is the deductible structure for a standard homeowners insurance policy, and does it differ by type of claim?

cases.name: flood and earthquake exclusions
cases.original: Does my homeowners insurance cover flood or earthquake damage?
adversarial: My basement flooded after a heavy rainstorm last week. I'm hoping my homeowners policy covers it. Does it?
So standard homeowners insurance covers water damage from internal sources but not flooding from outside the home, right?
if my house gets damaged in a flood or earthquake is that covered by regular home insurance
I just moved to an area that has some flood risk. I assumed I was covered but now I'm not sure. What exactly does my homeowners policy cover for weather events?
Are flood damage and earthquake damage covered under a standard homeowners insurance policy, or do they require separate policies?

cases.name: policy cancellation refund
cases.original: Can I cancel my insurance policy at any time and get a partial refund?
adversarial: I just found a cheaper insurer and I want to switch immediately. I paid 6 months upfront. Will I get back the unused portion if I cancel today?
So if I cancel mid-term, I get a prorated refund for the remaining coverage period minus any cancellation fee?
if i cancel my insurance before it expires do i get any money back
I prepaid for 6 months and I've only been covered for 2. I need to switch and I'm hoping I'm not just going to lose $200.
If a policyholder cancels their insurance mid-term, are they entitled to a refund for the unused portion of the premium?

cases.name: liability coverage scope
cases.original: What does my liability coverage actually protect me from?
adversarial: Someone slipped and fell on my property and they're talking about suing me. I need to understand exactly what my homeowners liability coverage would cover in this situation.
So my liability coverage pays for legal defense and any damages awarded up to my policy limits if someone is injured on my property and sues me?
what does liability insurance actually cover and what does it not cover
I'm genuinely scared about being sued. I need to understand in plain terms what my policy will and won't cover before I talk to anyone.
What situations and costs does personal liability coverage in a homeowners or renters policy typically cover?

cases.name: claim frequency limit
cases.original: Is there a limit to how many claims I can file in a year?
adversarial: I've had an unusually rough year and I've already filed two claims. Something else just happened. Am I going to have a problem filing a third claim this year?
So there's no hard cap on how many claims I can file, but filing multiple claims in a short period could affect my renewal or insurability?
can i file more than one insurance claim per year or is there a limit
I need to file another claim but I'm scared that filing too many will get my policy cancelled. What is the actual rule here?
Is there a formal limit on the number of insurance claims a policyholder can file annually, and what are the consequences of filing multiple claims?
